cited_work: Ten Arguments in Search of a Philosopher: Averroes and Aquinas in Ficino's Platonic Theology
relevant_topic: -P/-F distinction: Averroes's actual claim vs. Ficino's charge
claim_summary: Copenhaver introduces the -P/-F distinction: -P (Averroes's actual repeated claim) is that the Intellect is not a body or power in a body; -F (Ficino's charge against Averroes) is that the Intellect is not the substantial form of the body. Averroes holds -P but does not assert -F; the -F formulation is Aquinas's paraphrase, not Averroes's own language.
exact_quote: -P: the Intellect is not a body or a power in a body; -F: the Intellect is not the substantial form of the body; F: the intellect is the substantial form of the body. But Aquinas also seems to suggest that Averroes actually professes -F, which is incorrect, and Ficino follows him in this apparent misunderstanding.
notes: The -P/-F notation is Copenhaver's own terminological invention for this article. Central to the argument of the whole piece.
